page: Microsoft Azure E112iads_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7763 processor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 112 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 56 physical cores with multithreading enabled running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance is configured with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, providing 3891 GB of temporary NVMe disk capacity, and the instance supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory operations. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and PassMark CPU and memory scores. Cryptographic capabilities are also in the top 10% via the PassMark CPU encryption test, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instructions. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, though Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks fall into the middle 50% average tier.

While the small-block cached memory bandwidth is strong, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths are average, placing in the middle 50%. This suggests that workloads requiring massive sequential memory transfers may experience standard throughput, whereas cached, highly localized memory operations perform exceptionally well. The bundled 3891 GB of local storage provides high-speed temporary space, which, combined with strong database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, supports data-intensive applications.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and isolated hardware profile, the E112iads_v5 offers high resource density for enterprise workloads. It is suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, multi-threaded data decompression, and software compilation. The absence of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, though it maintains strong performance for small to medium LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E112iads_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 112 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 3891 GB of local NVMe storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded decompression, and memory scores, alongside strong cryptographic and database execution. However, single-core processing and large-block uncached memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated hardware isolation, this instance is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and intensive multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E112iads_v5 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 112 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and 3891 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An isolated, high-capacity memory and storage instance featuring 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7763 processors at 3.525 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory providing a high memory-to-core ratio
3891 GB of bundled local NVMe storage with Premium Storage support
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory, and cryptographic benchmark performance
Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded data processing
